events.label: The fleet turns east
events.txt: Ambrose Channel R"14" — the course's ONE mandatory mark — goes to port and the ocean reach begins. The leaders round about 90 minutes after their guns; from here it is 100+ nm of pressed reaching along the south shore.

events.label: A 31-footer among the 50s
events.txt: Mid-reach, Daffodil is holding the pace of the three fastest boats in the fleet — she will traverse the ocean leg 4th of 43 (8¼ h; fleet median 12 h), behind only Avalanche, Max and Wahoo, all far larger. This is what a Corsair F31R is for.

events.label: Montauk in the dark
events.txt: The mid-fleet rounds Montauk Point in the small hours (Sound's Great at 03:20, matching her crew's published account to within ten minutes); the leaders had turned before midnight. Next: Gardiners Bay, Plum Gut, and a Sound with fading wind.

events.label: The Sound turns upwind
events.txt: Friday morning's sou'wester sits 20-58 degrees off the westbound course (model reanalysis — no working wind platform existed in the central Sound in 2025) and dies from 11 kt toward 7. This beat is where Daffodil's race went: about 2 h conceded to Habiru YCC on overlapping water, with more tacks and 6 nm more sailed.

events.label: Line honors: Wahoo
events.txt: The Naval Academy's Ker 50 is first home in 24:11:49 — the fastest boat around the course by nearly two hours. On corrected time the handicap flips her behind Max by 4:50, a margin inside rating noise.

events.label: Max finishes — the door is still open
events.txt: Max crosses at 14:15, the third and last boat home before the squall line enters the Sound 80 minutes later — and hours before the post-frontal calm shuts the final 15 nm on everyone behind. The overall trophy is decided here, not in any margin.

events.label: Squall line enters the Sound
events.txt: Kings Point gusts 24.5 kt with a sharp direction reversal; NY Harbor 31.9 kt at 16:40; offshore Islip 23.5 kt at 17:30 — the NWS logged a severe line crossing Westchester/Fairfield into central Long Island 2:20-6:00 PM. Only three boats had finished; Max was the last of them, 80 minutes earlier.

events.label: The squall hits Daffodil
events.txt: About 30 kt felt for roughly two minutes (buoy 44065 recorded a 31.9 kt gust at 16:40). Main and jib down before/while it hit, jib-only after, then main back up — 'a very needed push' in air that was ~2 kt either side of it. Owner's account, corroborated by the buoy record and the track's near-stop at 16:20-16:50.

events.label: Anchored 175 yards from the line
events.txt: Sound's Great, becalmed at midnight, anchored ~0.09 nm from the finish for ~55 minutes until the new breeze arrived after 00:45 — the sharpest image of the finish door shut. Published account (Lenoble), confirmed by the tracker.
